result:
**Goal: Add a Neural Cellular Automata (NCA) simulation mode with Shift+N toggle.**

This is the natural next frontier for the project. Every current simulation uses hand-crafted rules, but Neural Cellular Automata — popularized by the "Growing Neural Cellular Automata" research — use a small learned update rule (approximated here with a configurable multi-layer perceptron) to produce self-organizing, self-repairing patterns. The mode would:

- Implement a lightweight NCA where each cell has continuous state channels updated by a small configurable kernel + activation function
- Include presets like **"Grow"** (pattern grows from a seed), **"Persist"** (pattern maintains shape against perturbation), **"Morphogenesis"** (Turing-like emergent structure), and **"Regenerate"** (self-repairs when you erase cells)
- Support interactive perturbation: the user can paint/erase cells and watch the system recover
- Use Sobel-filter perception (consistent with the seminal NCA paper) and a simple MLP update, all in pure NumPy

This fills a gap — it bridges the hand-designed rules (Game of Life, Lenia) with the learned-rule paradigm, and the self-repair behavior will be visually striking in the terminal. Want me to build it?
